none: Beadle-Greenway area plan : environmental impact statement.

The Beadle Greenway Area plan aims to revitalize an older, deteriorating section of Sioux Falls, South Dakota, located along the Big Sioux River. The plan proposes redeveloping the area into a residential neighborhood with up to 1100 dwelling units, preserving natural riverbank areas, and improving infrastructure. The goal is to enhance the area's recreational and natural value while minimizing pollution and social disruption. The redevelopment is expected to increase the tax base and provide more housing opportunities near downtown, but it also involves displacing some residents and businesses.

Sioux Falls, Minnehaha County, South Dakota
City of Sioux Falls Departments of Planning and Community Development
Sioux Falls Park and Recreation Department

The Beadle-Greenway area plan was proposed to address the decline of an older section of Sioux Falls, aiming to revitalize the area along the Big Sioux River. While the plan sought to enhance recreational spaces and improve infrastructure, it faced public pushback due to concerns over displacement of residents and businesses. Ultimately, the project aimed to balance development with environmental preservation and community needs.
United States. Department of Housing and Urban Development
United States. Department of Housing and Urban Development
"The expressed desire of elderly residents not to be forced from their homes lends support to the Maximum Conservation or Market Alternative." — Page 82

"We strongly believe that the Beadle area should be a residential neighborhood. Our main concerns in the Redevelopment of our area are the projected number of housing units and the projected population density." — BASCO/ACORN Response
